svg.text: WSL Ubuntu dry-run: guided setup
Tor Relay Setup 1.0.0-beta.3
Guided installer for public Guard/middle and exit relays.
It reviews privileged changes before applying them.
[01] +-- System Check
[01] | [OK] Accepted target: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS (noble, amd64)
[01] | [INFO] Would verify official Tor Project apt suite: https://deb.torproject.org/torproject.org/dists/noble/Release
[01] | [WARN] Dry-run mode is active. No packages or system files will be changed.
[01] | [INFO] Plain terminal mode selected.
[02] +-- System Hostname
[02] | [INFO] Current system hostname: windowssucks
[02] | [INFO] This is the Linux server name, not your public Tor relay nickname.
[02] | Change the system hostname before configuring Tor? [y/N]:
[03] +-- Relay Mode
[03] | [INFO] Guard/middle relays forward traffic inside the Tor network.
[03] | [INFO] Exit relays also connect Tor traffic to destinations on the public Internet, according to an exit policy.
[03] | Configure this relay as an exit relay? [y/N]:
[04] +-- Relay Identity
[04] | [INFO] Your nickname and ContactInfo will be public in Tor relay directories.
[04] | Relay nickname (1-19 letters/numbers): [04] | ContactInfo email or contact string: [04] | ORPort for incoming Tor connections [90
[05] | [INFO] Tor encourages IPv6 on relays, but only when IPv6 connectivity actually works.
[05] | Configure an additional IPv6 ORPort? [y/N]:
[06] +-- Relay Family
[06] | [INFO] Use MyFamily when you control more than one public Tor relay.
[06] | Do you control other Tor relays that should be in MyFamily? [y/N]:
[07] +-- Bandwidth and Traffic
[07] | [INFO] Tor AccountingMax is a hard quota and can hibernate the relay when exhausted.
[07] | [INFO] Steady monthly mode calculates RelayBandwidthRate and RelayBandwidthBurst from your VPS traffic budget.
[07] | Choose a bandwidth mode
[07] | 1) Steady monthly budget - Recommended for VPS traffic caps
[07] | 2) Manual rate and burst - Set RelayBandwidthRate/Burst yourself
[07] | 3) Hard monthly AccountingMax only - May hibernate when exhausted
[07] | 4) No relay-specific cap - Let Tor use available bandwidth
[07] | Choose [1]: [07] | [INFO] No relay-specific bandwidth cap will be written.
[08] +-- Maintenance Options
[08] | [INFO] Automatic security updates are strongly recommended for relay operators.
[08] | Configure unattended upgrades for security and Tor packages? [Y/n]: [08] | [WARN] You chose not to configure automatic updates. K
